summary: Focused verification of the deployment health-check fix. GET /health and GET /api/health both return 200 with {status:'ok'} on the pod's internal port 8001 (which is what the K8s ingress liveness/readiness probes actually hit). Backend startup shows 'Seed complete' 5x with no crash — the try/except around seed_all() works. Existing critical endpoints (login, dashboard stats, products, configure) all still 200, and the frontend /login still returns HTML. 9/9 tests pass.
startup_verification: backend.err.log tail shows 5 consecutive 'INFO: Application startup complete' preceded by 'INFO: Seed complete' — try/except around seed_all() confirmed working; no crash on restart.
note_on_public_health_url: Externally, https://<preview>/health returns 200 but serves the frontend React index.html because the ingress only routes /api/* to backend. This is expected — K8s liveness/readiness probes target the container port (8001) directly where /health correctly returns JSON {status:'ok'}. The original deployment failure (repeated 404 on /health) is fully resolved.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py uses deprecated @app.on_event('startup'); recommend migrating to FastAPI lifespan context manager (non-blocking, but will break in future FastAPI versions).

The public URL /health serves the frontend (ingress rule limitation). Not a bug for K8s probes (they use pod port), but if any external monitor pings /health via ingress it will get HTML — consider aliasing under /api/health for external checks (already added).
seed_data_creation: None — used existing seeded data (8 products, 4 customers, 3 partners).
context_for_next_testing_agent: The health-check deployment fix is fully verified. AI budget issue from iteration_1 (POST /api/ai/recommend returning 500 due to EMERGENT_LLM_KEY budget=0) is still open but out of scope for this iteration.
